taylor-williams.female_specific_note: Taylor Williams Coaching focuses on female-specific HRT/peptide guidance. Per-peptide notes here often emphasize female dosing, fertility, hormone interactions.

reset_biology_synthesis.recommendation: Reset Biology suggests asking the practitioner to consider Cartalax as a chondrocyte-targeted bioregulator rather than a general healing peptide, with Hunter (Bd2WVS6Jv70 00:01:51.920): "TB-500 on this list is because Cartalax absolutely blows those out of water, in my opinion, when it comes to healing" framing the case for it but Hunter email threadId 19be2cf9a8f0850c (2026-01-21) cautioning, "If the issue is acute injury, inflammation, or soft tissue damage, Cartalax may not be the primary tool to use in isolation. If the issue is joint aging, degeneration, or cartilage that never fully rebounds, Cartalax often becomes the missing piece." We'd ask the prescribing physician to weigh Hunter's published dose ladder — Hunter email threadId 1965f8635cb5e8f2 (2025-04-22): "Preventative Maintenance — 1 mg daily — 10 days — Every 4-6 months," "Mild to Moderate Issues — 2 mg daily — 20 days — Every 3-4 months," "Severe Issues / Post-Surgery — 2 mg daily — 30-60 days — Every 3-6 months" — and the route choice between subcutaneous (systemic) versus direct intra-articular injection that Hunter teaches in the same email: "Subcutaneous (Sub-Q) = systemic inflammation" / "Direct injection into problematic area = localized pain." Trevor Bachmeyer is silent on Cartalax in the indexed transcripts and emails, so the synthesis below leans on Hunter's primary content with a single Taylor anecdotal datapoint.
reset_biology_synthesis.caveats:
- Trevor silent: no Bachmeyer transcript or email mentions Cartalax in the indexed corpus — clinicians should treat Trevor's standard subQ/cycling doctrine as not yet specifically applied to this peptide.
- Acute-injury isolation caveat: Hunter email threadId 19be2cf9a8f0850c (2026-01-21): "If the issue is acute injury, inflammation, or soft tissue damage, Cartalax may not be the primary tool to use in isolation" — Hunter pairs it with BPC-157, TB-500, and GHK-Cu in the 4-peptide joint stack rather than running it alone for fresh injuries.
- Dose-ceiling tension: Hunter podcast (Bd2WVS6Jv70 00:02:02.160): "I will inject just 1 to 2 mg of Cartalax" matches his email ladder topping out at "2 mg daily" for severe cases (threadId 1965f8635cb5e8f2, 2025-04-22) — no expert in the corpus endorses higher daily dosing.
- Cycling/duration tension: Hunter email threadId 1965f8635cb5e8f2 (2025-04-22) caps even severe-issue courses at "30-60 days" with rest intervals "Every 3-6 months," while Hunter email threadId 1979ed3cdb566ab1 (2025-06-23) says "I typically run this protocol for 30 days consecutively" with "up to 90 days consecutively" only "If you are severely damaged" and maintenance "2-3 times per year" — newer 2026-01-21 email overrides nothing here, both ladders converge on cycled rather than continuous use.
- Route caveat: Hunter email threadId 1979ed3cdb566ab1 (2025-06-23): "Cartalax is usually used as an injectable, either systemically (for widespread joint pain) or locally (right into an injury or 'bad joint')" — oral use is not described; oral bioavailability is not addressed in the corpus and should not be assumed.
- Stacking caveat: Hunter email threadId 19be2cf9a8f0850c (2026-01-21) joint stack instructs "Cartalax: 1 mg/day" simultaneously with "BPC-157: 500 mcg/day," "TB-500: 500 mcg/day," "GHK-Cu: 2.5 mg/day" — separate syringes implied; co-administration with KPV in one syringe is anecdotal only per Taylor episode (Cg7JtpnfRY4 02:00:55.200): "I'm currently taking both. I have and have it's worked great" with the speaker immediately admitting (02:01:01.960): "I haven't tested those. I haven't got the third-party verified test."
- Allergy/safety claim: Hunter email threadId 1965f8635cb5e8f2 (2025-04-22): "No reported side effects. Metabolizes into harmless amino acids — no organ stress, no toxicity" and threadId 1979ed3cdb566ab1 (2025-06-23): "Neither product triggered allergies (thanks to the small size of the active peptide) or any weird reactions, even in older adults with other health issues" — sourced primarily to Russian Linkova et al. literature Hunter cites; Western RCT data is absent.
reset_biology_synthesis.cross_expert_consensus: Where Hunter and Taylor overlap is narrow but present. Hunter (Bd2WVS6Jv70 00:01:51.920): "TB-500 on this list is because Cartalax absolutely blows those out of water, in my opinion, when it comes to healing" and Hunter (Bd2WVS6Jv70 00:02:21.400): "if I could only pick one healing peptide, it would definitely be Cartalax." Taylor episode (Cg7JtpnfRY4 02:06:54.240): "They are not placebo effect in my opinion. I've seen nothing short of miraculous from Cartalax and KPV in my own life." Both surface Cartalax as a high-conviction healing tool from personal experience, both hedge with first-person framing ("in my opinion" / "in my own life"), and neither cites third-party verified data — Taylor episode (Cg7JtpnfRY4 02:01:01.960): "I haven't tested those. I haven't got the third-party verified test." Trevor is absent from the consensus column entirely.
reset_biology_synthesis.cross_expert_disagreement: There is no substantive Hunter-vs-Taylor doctrinal disagreement on Cartalax in the indexed corpus — both endorse it from personal experience and neither contradicts the other. The notable disagreement is internal to Hunter: Hunter podcast (Bd2WVS6Jv70 00:01:51.920): "Cartalax absolutely blows those out of water, in my opinion, when it comes to healing" (positioning Cartalax as superior to BPC-157 and TB-500 for healing) sits in tension with Hunter email threadId 19be2cf9a8f0850c (2026-01-21): "If the issue is acute injury, inflammation, or soft tissue damage, Cartalax may not be the primary tool to use in isolation" (which carves Cartalax out as a degenerative-cartilage tool, not an acute-injury tool). The 2026-04-18 podcast describes injecting Cartalax into a fresh muscle tweak — Hunter (Bd2WVS6Jv70 00:01:59.560): "anytime I pull a muscle or tweak something, I will inject just 1 to 2 mg of Cartalax" — while the 2026-01-21 email recommends pairing it inside a 4-peptide stack rather than soloing it for the same scenario. Both quotes are from the same expert within ~3 months and are not reconciled in the corpus. Trevor silent. Taylor does not contradict.

acute.dose: Hunter podcast (Bd2WVS6Jv70 00:02:02.160): "I will inject just 1 to 2 mg of Cartalax"; Hunter email threadId 1965f8635cb5e8f2 (2025-04-22) ladder: "Severe Issues / Post-Surgery — 2 mg daily — 30-60 days — Every 3-6 months."
acute.route: Hunter email threadId 1965f8635cb5e8f2 (2025-04-22): "Subcutaneous (Sub-Q) = systemic inflammation" / "Direct injection into problematic area = localized pain"; Hunter email threadId 1979ed3cdb566ab1 (2025-06-23): "injected subcutaneously into the belly for full-body/joint pain, or directly into a problem area (e.g., knee, elbow, shoulder) for localized, acute injuries."
acute.frequency: Hunter email threadId 1965f8635cb5e8f2 (2025-04-22): "2 mg daily" for both "Mild to Moderate Issues" and "Severe Issues / Post-Surgery."
acute.duration: Hunter email threadId 1965f8635cb5e8f2 (2025-04-22): "Severe Issues / Post-Surgery — 30-60 days"; Hunter email threadId 1979ed3cdb566ab1 (2025-06-23): "30 days consecutively, especially after an injury flare" and "If you are severely damaged, you can run up to 90 days consecutively."
acute.verbatim_citation: Hunter (Bd2WVS6Jv70 00:01:59.560): "anytime I pull a muscle or tweak something, I will inject just 1 to 2 mg of Cartalax, and it seems to completely eradicate all the pain I have within a couple days." Hunter email threadId 1965f8635cb5e8f2 (2025-04-22): "Severe Issues / Post-Surgery — 2 mg daily — 30-60 days — Every 3-6 months."

chronic.dose: Hunter email threadId 1965f8635cb5e8f2 (2025-04-22) ladder: "Preventative Maintenance — 1 mg daily — 10 days — Every 4-6 months" and "Mild to Moderate Issues — 2 mg daily — 20 days — Every 3-4 months"; Hunter email threadId 19be2cf9a8f0850c (2026-01-21) standalone: "1-2 mg per day subQ" for "30 consecutive days."
chronic.route: Hunter email threadId 19be2cf9a8f0850c (2026-01-21): "1-2 mg per day subQ" for the standalone focused course; Hunter email threadId 1979ed3cdb566ab1 (2025-06-23): "injected subcutaneously into the belly for full-body/joint pain."
chronic.frequency: Hunter email threadId 1965f8635cb5e8f2 (2025-04-22): "1 mg daily" (preventative) or "2 mg daily" (mild-moderate); Hunter email threadId 19be2cf9a8f0850c (2026-01-21): "1 mg/day" inside the 4-peptide joint stack alongside "BPC-157: 500 mcg/day," "TB-500: 500 mcg/day," "GHK-Cu: 2.5 mg/day."
chronic.duration: Hunter email threadId 1965f8635cb5e8f2 (2025-04-22): "Preventative Maintenance — 10 days — Every 4-6 months" / "Mild to Moderate Issues — 20 days — Every 3-4 months"; Hunter email threadId 19be2cf9a8f0850c (2026-01-21) standalone: "30 consecutive days" and joint stack: "4-8 weeks"; Hunter email threadId 1979ed3cdb566ab1 (2025-06-23) maintenance: "2-3 times per year."
chronic.verbatim_citation: Hunter email threadId 19be2cf9a8f0850c (2026-01-21): "Standalone Cartalax (focused course) — 1-2 mg per day subQ — 30 consecutive days — Drives cartilage-specific gene signaling + matrix normalization." Hunter email threadId 19be2cf9a8f0850c (2026-01-21): "COMBINED 4-PEPTIDE JOINT STACK (4-8 weeks) — Cartalax: 1 mg/day — BPC-157: 500 mcg/day — TB-500: 500 mcg/day — GHK-Cu: 2.5 mg/day." Hunter email threadId 1965f8635cb5e8f2 (2025-04-22): "Preventative Maintenance — 1 mg daily — 10 days — Every 4-6 months."
